news.title: Juve, huge bid for Svilar! Carnevali 🙅‍♂️ Frattesi, Roma-Moreira 🤑
news.lede: A rest day from on-field football, with the World Cup taking a 48-hour break before the semifinals. We’re in the final stretch, with France, Spain, England, and Argentina all truly just one step away from history. What never stops, however, is the transfer market, which is actually going through a very heated moment right now. Inter are the main protagonists, with the major difficulties in replacing Dumfries shaping the Nerazzurri’s market moves.

news.title: Spain, France, Argentina and England beware: Demons haunt the World Cup semi-finals
news.lede: The World Cup spotlight causes single games to live longer in the memory than any other. The remaining teams at this tournament face defining moments. World Cup games mean more. England have only ever played 79 games in the finals, which is to say, not much more than two Premier League seasons in the 76 years since they first entered.

news.title: Rodri: ‘France are one of the best teams here, in great form, but so are Spain. We can beat them’
news.lede: The Spain captain on his team’s chances in the World Cup semi-final, analysing every game so far and how to get the best out of Lamine Yamal. “I’m not that bad,” Rodrigo Hernández says. It is the morning after the 2026 World Cup’s 100th game and in a conference room at the Cotton Bowl where old posters line the walls and Spain are about to start their penultimate training session before the 101st match, their captain is doing the calculations. Even with the six in which he has played and all the travel, from Atlanta to Guadalajara and Dallas to Los Angeles and back, 9,000 miles so far, he reckons he has watched the “immense majority”.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' group stage and knockout stage fixtures and results
news.lede: Keep track of all the European sides' fixtures and results at the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concludes with the final in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Wednesday 17 June: W 4-2 vs Croatia (Dallas) Tuesday 23 June: D 0-0 vs Ghana (Boston) Saturday 27 June: W 2-0 vs Panama (New York) Wednesday 1 July (R32): W 2-1 vs DR Congo (Atlanta) Monday 6 July (R16): W 3-2 vs Mexico (Mexico City) Saturday 11 July (QF): W 2-1 aet vs Norway (Miami) Wednesday 15 July (SF): vs Argentina (Atlanta, 21:00).

news.title: World Cup scouting report: the lowdown on England’s semi-final opponents Argentina | Nick Ames
news.lede: Defending champions will pose a threat with Messi’s genius but their lack of width and energy in the midfield offers encouragement for Thomas Tuchel. Switzerland knew how to deal with Lionel Messi. They congested the centre of the pitch and made it impossible for him to find angles for threaded passes or rapier finishes. One of Messi’s trademark moves, when the tempo has slowed, is to go through the gears with a quick bounce pass off one of his teammates near the edge of the box.

news.title: USWNT to play No 1 ranked Spain in two October friendlies as 2027 World Cup approaches
news.lede: Matches will take place on 10 and 13 October. Washington DC and Chester, Pennsylvania to host. The top two teams in women’s soccer will meet in a pair of friendlies this fall, with the United States hosting Spain in Washington DC on 10 October and Chester, Pennsylvania three days later. The meeting pits the 2024 Olympic gold medalist United States against a Spain side who won the 2023 women’s World Cup.

news.title: France at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about France at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. France were runners-up last time and will have an eye on the gold medals at FIFA World Cup 2026. Didier Deschamps' side, led by captain Kylian Mbappé, topped Group I ahead of Norway, Senegal and Iraq to reach the knockout phase. They then beat Sweden in the round of 32, Paraguay in the last 16 and Morocco in the quarter-finals.

news.title: FIFA selling the field to be used for the World Cup final for $450 a piece
news.lede: FIFA, accused of charging high prices for this year's tournament in the United States, is selling the grass that will be used for the July 19 final at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey at $450 per piece. FIFA is selling the grass that will be used for the July 19 final at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey at $450 per piece.
